_description: Naive cascade (large, detach) — two identical U-Nets [48,96,192,384] ~17M each, ~34M total. Critical control for the thesis claim 'naive cascading fails even when total capacity exceeds a single large U-Net'. Total params (~34M) are comparable to or larger than the single large U-Net baseline (~31M). If this still fails to beat the large baseline, capacity cannot rescue the naive architecture — the failure is structural.
